No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Continued to July 7, 2026, per the second amended notice of motion. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

DEMURRER to ANSWER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-10-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ALEXANDER XUE's DEMURRER to ANSWER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Alexander Xue, in propria persona, Plaintiff.
Samuel Ray, Esq., of Colla & Ray LLP, (415) 579-1414, for Defendant.

The matter is argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Alexander Xue's Demurrer to Defendant Juniper Cafe's Answer to Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint OVERRULED.
The answer states adequate facts to support affirmative defenses, the affirmative defenses are not uncertain or incomprehensible, and the answer adequately alleges for purposes of the pleadings the effect of any agreements by plaintiff. Any uncertainty can be cured in discovery.

A proposed order has been submitted as instructed in the tentative ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Court Reporter: Roseleen O'Brien; CSR: 11670; T: (510) 871-1085; E: calendar@emerickfinch.com. (301/CVA)

MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-10-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ALEXANDER XUE'S MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Alexander Xue, in propria persona, Plaintiff.
Samuel Ray, Esq., of Colla & Ray LLP, (415) 579-1414, for Defendant.

The matter is argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's Motion to Strike Portions of Defendant's Answer is DENIED.
The first affirmative defense, failure to state a claim, is a recognized form of pleading. (Bracker v. American Nat. Food (1955) 133 Cal.App.2d 338, 339-340.) The eighth affirmative defense, mootness, adds facts concerning whether this action presents a live controversy. The ninth affirmative defense, that the action is frivolous and may be sanctionable under Code of Civil Procedure, sec. 128.7, is not a traditional affirmative defense, but it is not irrelevant, false, or improper within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure, sec. 436, subdivision (a). The motion to strike is further denied as to the prayer for relief. No cross-complaint is required to seek attorneys' fees or sanctions.

A proposed order has been submitted as instructed in the tentative ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Court Reporter: Roseleen O'Brien; CSR: 11670; T: (510) 871-1085; E: calendar@emerickfinch.com. (301/CVA)
